findings.summary: Public Phase 1 endpoints are deployed and verified against the live cranfield-v0 OpenSearch index.
Full Cranfield export is implemented, cranfield-v0 is loaded with 1,400 live documents, and live evaluation metrics were recorded.
Cloudflare Workers write scope was proven by secret upload and Worker deployment.
Refined prf-rerank is the best live Cranfield candidate, but cannot be promoted to a released architecture until BEIR transferability is measured.
Repeated live OpenSearch calls slowed reranker tuning; GEN-017 mitigated this with cached field-sum retrieval pools and offline PRF tuning.
GEN-019 generated real local Ollama embeddings and evaluated vector/hybrid retrieval, resolving the missing real-embedding evidence gap.
Ollama llama3.1:8b chat-model embeddings are not strong enough for promotion: best hybrid nDCG@10 0.3035 and binary nDCG@20 0.4343 remain below refined PRF.
GEN-020/GEN-021 LTR has ranking signal and high candidate-pool oracle headroom, but cross-validated metrics remain below refined PRF and in-sample gains must not be treated as promotion evidence.
Pointwise boosted-tree LTR improves graded cross-validation to nDCG@10 0.3216 but still misses refined PRF 0.3260, and binary cross-validation regresses versus the lexical LTR ablation.
BGE BAAI/bge-base-en-v1.5 is the first embedding path to beat refined PRF offline and in remote OpenSearch: best remote hybrid nDCG@10 0.3533 and binary nDCG@20 0.4926.
BGE-feature LTR beats refined PRF offline, with boosted-tree nDCG@10 0.3603, but the simpler BGE hybrid path is slightly better on binary nDCG@20 and easier to validate remotely.
Remote OpenSearch BGE hybrid is the best production-shaped Cranfield candidate, but it remains candidate-only until transferability, latency/cost, and public/default promotion policy are addressed.
GEN-024 added milestone search and explain endpoints so readers can compare key Cranfield architecture states through stable URLs without changing the public/default route.

residual_risks: Public endpoint p95 latency needs a broader sampled run beyond point checks.
Provider cost depends on the selected managed OpenSearch service plan and usage.
Cranfield-specific query tuning can overfit unless Phase 2 confirms transferability.
Cached retrieval pools can become stale when the index, analyzer, first-stage architecture, query set, or retrieve depth changes.
Local chat-model embeddings can be mistaken for dedicated retrieval embeddings unless provider, model, and text-profile metadata are preserved.
LTR in-sample results can be mistaken for validated improvements unless query-grouped cross-validation remains the promotion gate.
Pointwise boosted-tree tuning can improve in-sample metrics sharply without delivering enough held-out nDCG gain for promotion.
Remote BGE vector/hybrid Cranfield gains can be mistaken for production readiness before transferability and latency/cost evidence are collected.
